evals.name: compare-analytics-db
evals.prompt: Compare ClickHouse vs Apache Druid vs StarRocks for powering high-cardinality, real-time event analytics dashboards (sub-second queries) at a mid-size B2B SaaS. I need a cited recommendation I can show my team.
evals.expected_output: A cited report following the report template: exec summary with a clear recommendation, key findings each with inline citations to real fetched sources, a section on tradeoffs/where sources disagree, and a Sources list of URLs that were actually fetched.
assertions: Covers all three systems (ClickHouse, Druid, StarRocks)
Gives a clear recommendation (a winner or an explicit conditional recommendation)
Has a Sources section listing >=3 distinct real URLs
Uses inline citation markers tied to claims (not a bare link dump)
Has a tradeoffs / where-sources-disagree / caveats section
Has an executive summary that leads with the answer

evals.name: health-evidence-brief
evals.prompt: Give me a cited research brief on whether creatine supplementation improves cognitive performance in healthy adults. Be honest about how strong the evidence actually is.
evals.expected_output: A cited brief that synthesizes primary sources, characterizes evidence strength, surfaces disagreement, separates evidence from inference, and flags weak/single-source claims. Real fetched citations only.
assertions: Cites primary/authoritative sources (studies, meta-analyses, or reviews) with >=3 real URLs
Explicitly characterizes strength of evidence (e.g. limited / mixed / moderate / strong)
Notes disagreement or heterogeneity across studies rather than one-sided claims
Separates what evidence shows from inference, or flags weak/single-source claims
Uses inline citations tied to specific claims

evals.name: clarify-underspecified
evals.prompt: research the best programming language for me
evals.expected_output: The skill's CLARIFY stage: 2-3 targeted scoping questions rather than a generic from-memory answer or an immediate full search.
assertions: Asks clarifying questions rather than producing a full ranked answer
Questions target scope/use-case/constraints/experience level (2-3 targeted questions)
Does NOT immediately dump a generic from-memory 'top languages' list as the final answer
